rag_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure that all identifiers used in assume/guarantee expressions are visible in the current AADL scope and properly declared.
Place output range guarantees in component type scopes only when concrete bounds or visible relations are provided; avoid assuming ranges without explicit definitions.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same AGREE annex to prevent unresolved identifier errors.
Align helper declaration types with the types of the expressions they represent to avoid type mismatch diagnostics.
Do not encode AADL property associations (e.g., timing or resource properties) as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, and `const` with explicit type annotations.
Output range guarantees should relate visible outputs to valid input or constant bounds.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing AADL package-qualified identifiers or property names that are not visible in the current scope.
Do not assume that AADL timing or resource properties are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
Implementation-level bindings or subcomponent port usage in type contracts
